---
title: "RP 6: Staying Busy with Purpose"
format: html
---
# RP 6: Staying Busy with Purpose

Unstructured time is a common relapse risk because boredom and rumination can increase craving. Staying busy is not about constant productivity; it is about creating enough meaningful structure to reduce high-risk gaps in the day.

Activities are most protective when they include a mix of responsibility, enjoyment, movement, and social connection. Recovery research supports behavioral activation: doing healthy activities first, then allowing mood and motivation to catch up.

Behavioral activation research shows that planned activity can improve mood and reduce craving vulnerability even before motivation returns. Small, consistent structure often outperforms ambitious plans that are hard to sustain.

## Structured Day Builder
| Time block | Planned activity | Category (responsibility, enjoyable, movement, connection) |
|---|---|---|
| Morning |  |  |
| Afternoon |  |  |
| Evening |  |  |

::: {.callout-caution}
### When risk is higher

If urges or stress rise quickly, use this sequence early rather than waiting.

Follow your plan for three days and adjust one part that did not work.

Early action usually prevents the cycle from becoming harder to interrupt.
:::
